questions.question: State and explain Theorem 10.1.
questions.answer: The tangent at any point of a circle is perpendicular to the radius through the point of contact.

questions.question: State and explain Theorem 10.2.
questions.answer: The lengths of tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal.

questions.question: Explain the proof of equality of tangents using RHS congruence.
questions.answer: Right triangles formed satisfy RHS conditions leading to equality.

questions.question: Why is perpendicular distance the shortest?
questions.answer: Any other distance is longer than the perpendicular.

questions.question: Explain why centre lies on angle bisector of tangents.
questions.answer: Because OP bisects the angle between two equal tangents.

questions.question: Prove that tangents drawn at the ends of a diameter are parallel.
questions.answer: Both tangents are perpendicular to the same diameter.

questions.question: Explain why tangent never becomes a secant.
questions.answer: Because it touches the circle at only one point.

questions.question: Why is tangent contact always unique?
questions.answer: Only one perpendicular can be drawn at a point.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between tangent and normal.
questions.answer: Normal is the radius perpendicular to the tangent.

questions.question: Why does tangent geometry rely on right triangles?
questions.answer: Because radius and tangent form right angles.

questions.question: Explain why two tangents are symmetric.
questions.answer: They are equal in length from the external point.

questions.question: Why is tangent important in circle constructions?
questions.answer: Because of fixed perpendicular relationships.

questions.question: Explain why tangent cannot pass through centre.
questions.answer: The centre lies inside the circle.

questions.question: Explain tangent properties using wheel example.
questions.answer: Wheel touches ground at exactly one point.

questions.question: Why are radii equal in all tangent proofs?
questions.answer: All radii of the same circle are equal.

questions.question: Explain the geometric rigidity of tangents.
questions.answer: Due to fixed perpendicular constraints.

questions.question: Why is tangent theorem fundamental in circles?
questions.answer: It defines radius-tangent relationship.

questions.question: Explain why tangent length is measurable.
questions.answer: It is a finite line segment.

questions.question: Why is centre equidistant from points of contact?
questions.answer: Because distances are radii.

questions.question: Explain why tangent never intersects interior.
questions.answer: It touches only the boundary.

questions.question: Explain why perpendicularity is crucial in tangents.
questions.answer: It establishes right-angle geometry.

questions.question: Explain the role of centre in tangent theorems.
questions.answer: Centre forms equal radii and right angles.

questions.question: Why does tangent theory use congruence?
questions.answer: To prove equality of lengths.

questions.question: Explain why tangent problems are solvable.
questions.answer: Because of fixed geometric relations.

questions.question: Explain the importance of tangents in circle geometry.
questions.answer: They define contact and boundary behavior.
